content:
{
  "title": "Bias-Decomposition Prompting: Controlling Social Stereotype Expression in LLMs via Structured Prompt Tokens",
  "problem": "Large language models often exhibit social biases and stereotypes, even in seemingly neutral responses. Existing prompt-based mitigation techniques are limited in their ability to isolate and control these biases during inference.",
  "motivation": "Reducing social bias in LLM outputs is crucial for trustworthy, ethical AI applications. Prompt-based methods are attractive due to their low overhead and model-agnostic nature, but current approaches often lack fine-grained control. A more structured prompting method could allow explicit specification of stereotype suppression without extensive retraining.",
  "method": "We propose Bias-Decomposition Prompting: for each input, prepend a structured prompt token (e.g., '<NO-STEREOTYPE>') or a short, natural-language tag instructing the model to suppress stereotype-laden content specifically. Prompt templates are automatically generated by analyzing model outputs on bias benchmark datasets and extracting frequent stereotype trigger patterns. During inference, the LLM receives the structured prompt and the user query. The hypothesis is that these tokens, when systematically derived, direct the model’s attention away from known bias modes.",
  "experiment_plan": "Use the StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs datasets for evaluation. Compare outputs using baseline prompts, generic debiasing prompts, and our auto-derived structured prompts. Metrics include stereotype score reduction and language fluency preservation. A/B test whether the proposed method reduces bias more effectively than generic anti-bias prompting."
}
